Patient states she will think about quitting smoking, indicating engagement in smoking-cessation behavior/intent.
Clinician states the patient is here for a follow-up visit.
Patient reports she hasn't had pasta and doesn't keep it in the house, an observation about dietary intake.
Patient reports she doesn't often have bread (and buys wheat bread if she does), an observation about dietary intake.
Patient reports buying wheat bread when she has bread; clinician discusses cutting down slices, reflecting dietary observation.
Clinician advises cutting down the number of wheat bread slices per day, documenting an observation/target about consumption amount.
Patient reports she usually has two slices of bread in the morning, an observation about intake amount and timing.
Patient reports she doesn't care for sandwiches much, an observation about dietary habits.
Patient reports having peanut butter sandwiches once in a while, an observation about intake frequency.
Patient reports current low soda intake ("hardly drink any sodas").
"Eyes" here is mentioned as a body site in the context of an iodine treatment/prescription, not clearly a specific condition the patient has.
Cataracts are discussed as the reason for eye treatment/surgery, implying the patient has/had cataracts.
Clinician asks if patient is trying to walk; patient answers yes, indicating she is doing walking activity.
Patient confirms she is still smoking.
Clinician initiates a review of systems as part of the visit for the patient.

Patient denies dizziness in response to a direct ROS question.

Patient denies shortness of breath when asked directly.

Cardiac exam findings (regular rhythm, normal rate, no murmurs) are documented for the patient.
Foot/extremity exam findings are documented for the patient (no edema, palpable pulses, thickened nails).
During the physical exam the clinician states the extremities are without edema, indicating absence of edema for the patient.
Clinician observes and states the patient has thickened nails on exam.
Clinician notes mildly dry skin on exam, indicating the patient has dry skin.
Clinician explicitly notes 'no skin breakdown' on exam, indicating absence of skin breakdown for the patient.
Clinician documents mildly decreased sensation in the tips of the first and second toes, consistent with numbness/decreased sensation of toes in the patient.
Patient reports using a foot scrub/cream on her feet after the shower (intermittently), indicating use of this topical product.
Patient mentions her 'girl' (a friend/person she talks to), which is captured as an observation associated with the patient.
Clinician states the patient's sugars are not well controlled and references A1c/blood sugar average above nine, supporting poor glycemic control as a patient condition.
Clinician plans to recheck the patient's blood work today, indicating an associated lab test for the patient.
